# Transcrição: My_thoughts_on_Joe_Rogan_and_Andrew_Tate

## [00:00 - 02:09]

I made a video where one of the people, and I listened to a lot of different podcasts, and one of the podcasts that I really enjoy is Joe Rogan. I like his podcast. And then on that particular one where I mentioned that I listened to Joe Rogan, I saw a bunch of comments, we go, oh, Vin, it's so unfortunate. I'm going to have to unfollow you now. And I remember looking at that with a lot of love and compassion, I kind of looked at it and went, oh, that's so interesting. It doesn't matter how much value I can bring that particular person, just because I have a certain taste. That means that person that they can no longer follow me. I mean, if you just extrapolate this into a type of food, let's say, for example, I liked eating green curry and you kind of worked out that I like green curry and you didn't like green curry, you're kind of saying that we can't hang out anymore because I like green curry and you don't. I don't think that's a very deep way of looking at it. I think that's kind of a very shallow way of looking at it. I think to every single human being, there's a good part of them and there's a bad part of them. We don't have to take all of it. We can just take some of it. There's good and bad to everything and life is really, really great. There are so many intricate topics of conversation that we are afraid to navigate. And as a result of being afraid to navigate some of the difficult conversations, we just kind of put things in buckets and it's, oh, it's either this or that. And then the easy thing to do is my team versus your team, as opposed to what Neil deGrasse Tyson says, which I love, and he says, we are all a part of the human team. I guess I'm just saying, I really want to be a voice on social media where it pushes us to be able to maybe have some difficult conversations and navigate them. Not look at it so simplistically, but rather sit and think more deeply about some difficult topics. Someone just brought up, what's your opinion on Andrew Tate? It doesn't matter who we talk about. If we start with the foundation that every person has good in them and every person has bad in them, even me, there's good about Andrew Tate and there's bad about Andrew Tate. There are some things he does I don't agree with, but there are some things he does. That's really cool. And I give myself permission to be able to hold those two ideas in my head, as opposed to just think that person is just bad and then I don't acknowledge any of the good in them. Whenever one asks me that question, you know, it's, it's, what's your opinion of that person or that person? I think sometimes they're looking for which side are you on? And for me, I'm just, I'm on the human side.
